Edinburgh is one of the most atmospheric cities in Europe. 🏴✨ Capital of Scotland, it’s a city shaped by medieval streets, volcanic hills, dramatic viewpoints and centuries of history. Its Old Town and New Town are both part of a UNESCO World Heritage Site, showing two very different sides of the city: the dark, narrow closes of the Old Town and the elegant Georgian architecture of the New Town. The castle dominates the skyline from Castle Rock, while the Royal Mile connects some of the most historic parts of the city. Around every corner you find stone façades, hidden courtyards, old pubs, bookshops and viewpoints that make Edinburgh feel almost cinematic. Places like Victoria Street, Dean Village, Calton Hill, The Vennel and Circus Lane show why this city is so special: it’s not just beautiful, it has character. Edinburgh is moody, elegant, literary, historic and unforgettable — one of those places that feels different from anywhere else in the UK. 🖤 #Edinburgh #Scotland #VisitScotland #EdinburghCastle #EuropeTravel

DAY 59/60: The Life of a Student Videographer I post all my videos in 5:4 (1350×1080), and it’s become my favorite format. I love the cinematic look of horizontal videos, but vertical performs better because it fills more of the screen. 5:4 is the perfect middle ground—it keeps the horizontal feel while taking up more space in the feed. It also lets me shoot everything horizontally without rotating my camera and simply crop in during editing. It’s the best of both worlds. Follow me, to be part of my journey  #videography #students #cinematographers #series #videographytips
